findings.message: `test_resolved_log_level_prod_is_info` is an exact duplicate of the existing parametrized case `test_resolved_log_level_is_info_outside_dev[prod]` (line 17–19 in the full file). Both call `Settings(environment="prod").resolved_log_level` and assert `== "INFO"`. They are fully coupled: any regression that breaks one breaks the other, so the new test contributes zero additive coverage. The 'data-leak' comment does not change the code path exercised.
findings.suggestion: Remove `test_resolved_log_level_prod_is_info`. If the security rationale ('never DEBUG in prod') merits explicit documentation, add a comment to the existing `@pytest.mark.parametrize` block — do not duplicate the assertion.

findings.message: `test_slo_request_latency_p99_env_override` exercises the same mechanism already covered by `test_env_vars_override` (which already asserts a `float` field — `slo_error_rate_pct` — is overridden from env). pydantic-settings applies the same coercion uniformly to every scalar field; a second single-field float override test adds no new failure mode. The only distinct value would be verifying the exact env-var-name-to-attribute mapping for this specific field.
findings.suggestion: Either (a) fold this assertion into `test_env_vars_override` as an additional `setenv`/`assert` line alongside the existing float check, or (b) keep it only if the field-name mapping (`APP_SLO_REQUEST_LATENCY_P99_MS` → `.slo_request_latency_p99_ms`) is a documented contract requirement worth a dedicated test.
